page: Amazon Web Services c8ine.large is a compute-optimized instance belonging to the c8ine server family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by dedicated Intel processors running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 virtual CPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance is optimized for network and Elastic Block Store performance. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached EBS volumes.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8ine.large offers a dedicated compute profile tailored for workloads requiring high clock speeds with a lean memory footprint. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand large memory capacities. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, batch processing, and distributed compute nodes.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ine.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by dedicated 3.9 GHz Intel processors. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 4.0 GB of system memory. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps and does not include local storage or GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It is designed for compute-heavy workloads with low memory requirements, such as batch processing, lightweight web serving, and distributed compute tasks.
